changes.description: Homepage headline now positions as 'AI-powered client relationship platform' — heavy AI emphasis with dedicated AI section featuring email drafts, project recaps, business trends, and meeting notetaker
changes.new_value: AI-powered client relationship platform with AI email drafts, project recaps, business trends, meeting notetaker
changes.strategic_note: HoneyBook is aggressively differentiating on AI. Argo should consider how to position its own AI capabilities or counter-position on photographer-specific depth.

changes.description: Three-tier pricing confirmed: Starter $29/mo, Essentials $49/mo (was $59, now 25% off), Premium $109/mo (was $129). All billed yearly.
changes.new_value: Starter $29/mo | Essentials $49/mo | Premium $109/mo (annual billing)
changes.strategic_note: HoneyBook's 25% discount on mid/premium tiers suggests competitive pressure. Argo can undercut on price while emphasizing photographer-specific features.

changes.description: Published product updates post for March 2026 highlighting new payment methods and file-building improvements. Heavy case study output — celebrity cake artist, social media agencies, beauty studios, elopement photographers, consultants.
changes.previous_value: Blog focused on general business tips
changes.new_value: Aggressive case study / success story publishing across multiple verticals (not just photographers)
changes.strategic_note: HoneyBook is broadening beyond photographers to all creative/service businesses. This is both a threat (bigger brand) and opportunity (less photographer-specific content).

changes.description: Homepage focuses on three verticals: Licensed & Specialized Services, Creatives, and Management/Events/Coordination. Added 'Business financing' feature (Dubsado Payments Capital).
changes.new_value: Three-vertical positioning with business financing (Payments Capital) as new feature
changes.strategic_note: Dubsado adding business financing is a significant value-add. They're becoming a financial partner, not just a CRM.

changes.description: Two plans: Starter $335/yr (~$28/mo), Premier $525/yr (~$44/mo). Additional brands $10/mo. Users: 4-10 at $25/mo, 11-20 at $45/mo.
changes.new_value: Starter $335/yr | Premier $525/yr | Add-on brands $10/mo
changes.strategic_note: Dubsado is price-competitive with HoneyBook. Starter lacks automations/scheduling — similar gating strategy.

changes.description: Launched Dubsado 3.0 — described as 'faster, more intuitive platform'. Ambassador program now active.
changes.new_value: Dubsado 3.0 with redesigned UI + Ambassador program
changes.strategic_note: Major platform refresh signals renewed investment. Ambassador program builds word-of-mouth moat.

changes.description: Strong content output: pricing guides, finding first clients, niche-finding, freelancing state report, discovery call questions, client onboarding. Emphasis on helping brand-new businesses.
changes.new_value: Heavy beginner-focused content targeting early-stage freelancers and creative businesses
changes.strategic_note: Dubsado is capturing top-of-funnel beginners. Argo should target the same audience with photographer-specific beginner content.

changes.description: Tave has been fully absorbed into VSCO Workspace. tave.com redirects to vsco.co/workspace. Positioned as 'Smart Tools to Run and Grow Your Business'.
changes.new_value: Fully rebranded as VSCO Workspace — tave.com redirects to vsco.co/workspace
changes.strategic_note: CRITICAL CHANGE: Tave brand is effectively dead. VSCO Workspace now backed by VSCO's brand recognition among photographers. This is a bigger competitor than Tave was alone.

changes.description: Three tiers: Solo $22.49/mo (annual), Boutique $31.49/mo, Studio $44.99/mo. Includes SMS texting, phone support, free onboarding, and CRM migration support.
changes.new_value: Solo $22.49/mo | Boutique $31.49/mo | Studio $44.99/mo (annual). Free onboarding + CRM migration.
changes.strategic_note: VSCO Workspace offers free CRM migration — directly targeting users of competitors. Aggressive customer acquisition play.

changes.description: tave.com/pricing returns 410 'Brand Not Found' — migration of old Tave URLs appears incomplete
changes.new_value: 410 error on old Tave URLs
changes.strategic_note: Opportunity: Tave users searching for tave.com/pricing will hit dead links. Argo could create 'Tave alternatives' or 'VSCO Workspace alternatives' content to capture confused users.

changes.description: Single all-inclusive plan: $60/mo, $600/yr, $800/2yr. Currently running 50% off first purchase promo.
changes.new_value: $60/mo | $600/yr | $800/2yr (50% off first purchase promo active)
changes.strategic_note: 17hats is the most expensive monthly option. Single plan simplifies decision but limits entry-level appeal. Pricing page URL changed (old /pricing.html returns 404).

changes.description: Positioning as 'CRM + Business Management Platform for Solo Business Owners'. Emphasizes 25,000+ users and financial growth milestones.
changes.new_value: Solo business owner focus, 25K+ users, glass ceiling / growth messaging
changes.strategic_note: 17hats targets solo operators exclusively — not teams. Smaller competitor but owns a niche.

changes.description: Positioned as 'Pixieset Photographer Platform' — all-in-one with galleries, website, CRM. Claims 1M+ users, 6B+ photos delivered, $1.5B+ sold. Now includes Studio Manager for invoices/documents.
changes.new_value: Full photographer platform: galleries + website + Studio Manager (CRM/invoicing). 1M+ users.
changes.strategic_note: Pixieset expanding from galleries into full business management is a direct competitive threat to Argo. Their gallery dominance gives them a massive installed base to upsell.

changes.description: Gallery-focused pricing: Free ($0), Basic $8/mo, Plus $16/mo, Pro $24/mo, Ultimate $40/mo. Studio Manager pricing appears separate/bundled.
changes.new_value: Free → $8 → $16 → $24 → $40/mo (gallery tiers). Studio Manager bundled.
changes.strategic_note: Pixieset's free tier is a massive funnel. Hard to compete on price but easy to compete on depth of business management features.

changes.description: Rebranded messaging as 'Emotional Support Software for Photographers' — quirky, community-focused positioning. Emphasizes $0 commission, $1B+ in user sales, 400K+ photographers.
changes.new_value: Emotional Support Software for Photographers. $0 commission. 400K+ users.
changes.strategic_note: ShootProof's emotional branding is distinctive and memorable. They're competing on community and support, not features. Hard to replicate but easy to out-feature.

changes.description: Storage-based tiers: 100 photos (free trial), 1,500 photos, 5,000 photos, 25,000 photos, Unlimited. All include unlimited galleries, contracts, invoicing. Specific dollar amounts require JS rendering.
changes.new_value: Photo-count tiers from 100 to Unlimited. All plans include contracts, invoicing, selling tools.
changes.strategic_note: Storage-based pricing is unique in this market. Good for portfolio photographers who need lots of storage.

changes.description: Added online client booking, questionnaires (coming soon), and mobile gallery app across all tiers. Google Calendar integration.
changes.new_value: Now includes booking, contracts, invoicing, questionnaires (soon), mobile app
changes.strategic_note: ShootProof following Pixieset's playbook — expanding from galleries into full business management. Gallery-first platforms converging with CRM-first platforms.

changes.description: Positioned as 'All-in-one business workspace & CRM for pro services'. Not photographer-specific — targeting all creative professionals. Heavy influencer/educator endorsements (Chris Do, Matt Gray, Alexandra Fasulo).
changes.new_value: All-in-one workspace for pro services. Endorsed by major business educators/influencers.
changes.strategic_note: Bloom.io has strong influencer partnerships driving awareness. Pricing page didn't render content (JS-heavy) — may need browser visit for details.

changes.description: Site returned 403 Forbidden on both homepage and pricing page — possible geo-blocking, bot protection, or site issues.
changes.new_value: 403 Forbidden — site inaccessible via automated fetch
changes.strategic_note: Need browser-based visit to confirm status. If Sprout Studio is having issues or shutting down, this creates opportunity to capture their users.

changes.description: Multiple blog posts tracked — finances, client acquisition, product updates, brochure creation, case studies across verticals
changes.new_value: 17 blog posts cataloged
changes.strategic_note: High content velocity across diverse topics

changes.description: Multiple blog posts tracked — lead capture, goal-setting, work-life balance, freelancing report, ambassador program, pricing guides, client onboarding
changes.new_value: 18 blog posts cataloged
changes.strategic_note: Strong beginner-focused content strategy
